openingQuery: The linen throw I bought 4 months ago developed a tear at the seam — I know the standard window closed but pieces like this should last. Can you handle this as an exception?

chat.content: The linen throw I bought 4 months ago developed a tear at the seam — I know the standard window closed but pieces like this should last. Can you handle this as an exception?

You're right — slow-craft linen shouldn't fail at the seam after four months. The standard return path can't accept an exception this far past the window, so I've routed your note to a Pellier stylist with the full context. They'll reach out within one business day to take it from here. Keep the throw; we'll tell you what to do with it.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → Experience Guide → escalate_to_stylist (exception beyond Cedar policy)

toolCalls.description: Hand off to a human after process_return cannot accommodate an out-of-window exception
toolCalls.resultSummary: Stylist handoff queued · response window 1 business day

memoryPills.content: Bought Linen Throw, Oat ($118) 47d ago — purchase confirmed but past window
Cedar refuses process_return beyond return window → escalate_to_stylist

meta.note: Cedar policy enforces the return window. process_return refuses beyond it. escalate_to_stylist is the honest exit for durability-expectation exceptions a human should weigh.

brief.headline: Cedar said no. The honest fallback said 'a person'.

sections.title: Why process_return refuses
paragraphs: Theo's prior turn (the chipped Wabi-Sabi Bowl) ran cleanly through process_return because Cedar's policy allows the 'damaged' reason inside the standard window. Here, four months have passed — the same Cedar policy refuses the write, and SQL would refuse it too. The automated path is closed.
But the case isn't closed. A linen throw with a seam failure at four months is a durability claim, not a buyer's remorse. That's a judgment call, and judgment calls belong with a person.

sections.title: Honest fallback over silent failure
paragraphs: The Experience Guide's system prompt names this exact case: 'Use ONLY when process_return cannot handle the case — Cedar rejected the reason, the customer doesn't own the product, the window has closed, or the shopper is in distress and deserves a real person.'

escalate_to_stylist emits an envelope with the customer's framing intact. The stylist sees the full context — purchase date, durability expectation, shopper's tone — and decides. The customer is not stranded with a refusal; they are routed to a person who can say yes or no.
